title: GlassBox execution trace
description: The single schema for everything the player eats. A traced Python program is 'execution-derived'; a hand-authored process model is 'author-asserted'. validate-traces.mjs additionally cross-checks derivation_source against provenance.trace_source and the per-step provenance.

derivation_source.description: How the STATE was produced: did CPython actually run it? (`execution-derived` = sys.settrace; `author-asserted` = hand-authored; `hybrid` = mixed).

domain_model.description: Orthogonal to derivation_source: the program's relationship to its real-world subject. `execution-derived` = the program IS the subject (a swap is a swap; place-value is place-value — no metaphorical gap). `author-asserted-simulation` = a real-but-toy model standing in for a larger real system (a routing sim: the trace is real, the claim that it models real routing is the author's). `real-world-data` = the trace replays captured external data. A trace can be execution-derived AND an author-asserted-simulation at once — running a model perfectly does not validate the model.

event.description: `line`, `call`, and `return` are emitted today; `print` and `exception` are reserved for future tracer events (forward-compatible, like `checkpoint`).

frame.description: Name of the function whose locals `state` shows. Present only inside a function (depth > 0) or on a call/return event; absent at module level.

depth.description: Call depth: 0 = module level, 1 = inside a called function, etc.

return_value.description: The value handed back to the caller. Present on `return` events; any JSON value, or a tagged {__repr__} placeholder.

state.description: Full snapshot of the current frame's in-scope variables. Values are JSON primitives/arrays/objects, a tagged {__repr__, __unserializable__} placeholder for non-JSON-native values, a {__ref__: <id>} back-edge for a cyclic container, or a {__map__: true, entries: [[key, value], …]} list for a dict with non-string keys.

refs.description: Maps a variable path to a small object id, present only where identity carries information: a mutable container shared by two names in THIS step (aliasing) or the target of a {__ref__} cycle back-edge. Equal ids in one step mean the SAME object, not merely equal values.

note.description: Author-asserted meaning for this step (a claim, not an execution fact).

value_flags.description: Maps a variable path to the reason its value was coerced (e.g. repr-coerced, non-finite-float, tuple-as-array).

checkpoint.description: Gate forward motion here and ask the learner to predict the NEXT step's state before revealing it. The real next state is the sole adjudicator.

checkpoint_prompt.description: Optional author-posed question shown at a checkpoint — the question only, never the answer.
